title: Elsa speaks app
review:
I found the app experience frustrating almost immediately. The AI avatar’s voice sounded robotic and unpleasant, and there was no option to change it. This is a significant drawback, especially for a product designed for frequent, long-term use.

The lesson structure also felt rigid. I attempted to change the topic from “landmarks,” but the app ignored the request and continued with the preset lesson. This lack of flexibility made the experience feel unnatural and limiting.

More concerning was the accuracy of the speech recognition. When I said “State Capitol of Texas,” the app repeatedly transcribed it as “state capital of Texas” and then incorrectly indicated that it was not a landmark. This kind of error undermines confidence in the app’s ability to provide reliable feedback, especially when distinguishing between commonly confused terms like “capitol” and “capital.”

Overall, I appreciate that there is a free trial, as it allowed me to evaluate the app before committing. However, after only a few minutes of use, I could not see myself using it consistently, let alone over the course of a full year.

title: Latest Update Ruined the App
review:
I am extremely disappointed with the latest update.

I purchased a yearly subscription based on the previous version of the app, which was simple, clear, and actually helpful for learning.

Now the entire concept has changed. The app relies heavily on AI, but it does not work properly. Speech recognition is inaccurate, even for very simple words, which makes it frustrating and unusable.

The previous version had clear guidance, translations, and structured practice. Now all of that is gone. There are no helpful hints, and the conversations are far too difficult for the level I selected.

Instead of improving the app, this update has made it significantly worse. Learning has become stressful instead of enjoyable.

This is not the product I paid for. If I had known the app would change like this, I would never have purchased a subscription.

I cannot recommend this app anymore.

title: Very Disappointed After the Latest Update – Long-Time User
review:
I have been using Elsa Speak for about 4 years, and until today, I was genuinely satisfied with the app. It helped me improve my pronunciation and build confidence in speaking English.

However, after the latest update, I am extremely disappointed. I spent around two hours trying to use the new version, and it feels completely different — and not in a good way.

The app is now overloaded with AI assistants that appear everywhere. Instead of making the learning experience smoother, they make it confusing and distracting. The interface feels cluttered, and it’s harder to focus on structured learning.

Even worse, some of the courses that were important to me seem to have disappeared. I used those courses regularly, and now I can’t find them. That was one of the main reasons I kept my subscription.

I don’t understand why such drastic changes were made without keeping the core learning structure intact. At this point, I cannot recommend updating the app. If things don’t improve, I may seriously consider stopping my subscription after four years of loyalty.

I hope the developers listen to long-term users and reconsider these changes.

title: Learning App? More Like Loading App!
review:
I paid for a full year of membership but only used it for a few weeks. I got so frustrated and angry using it. First of all, the user experience is not friendly at all. Everything takes forever to load — and no, my internet is fine; all my other apps work perfectly.

Second, it’s super annoying that when you tap the button to speak, you have to wait a long time and can only start talking after a beep.

Third, the learning units are fixed. They make you pick a topic, but you still have to go through their default units with objects you don’t care about. And because each unit is quite short, it keeps loading the next one so often that the time you spend waiting for it to load feels equal to the time you actually spend learning.

What a waste of money and time.

He seguido todos los pasos: restaurar compra, reinstalar la app, contactar soporte (¡sin respuesta durante meses!)… y nada. Peor aún, las funciones por las que pagué, como preparación para el TOEFL y el examen de ciudadanía, siguen bloqueadas. ELSA está vendiendo un producto que no entrega lo prometido. Si valoran su tiempo, dinero y confianza, NO compren esta aplicación. Es una decepción y una falta de respeto al usuario.

When I ask a real English teacher for feedback on the same phrases, they confirm that my pronunciation is correct, but ELSA often scores me at 60 or 70. title: Paid App Plagued by Bugs, Ads, and Terrible Voice Detection
review:
This app started with promise, but quickly devolved into a frustrating experience that feels like a complete rip-off. The core functionality, voice detection, is shockingly unreliable. Even in a perfectly quiet, closed room, with absolutely no background noise, the app frequently fails to register my voice. This critical flaw makes the app's primary purpose practically useless.
Worse, despite paying for the 'premium' version, I'm constantly bombarded with ads to pay for the app again. It's incredibly annoying and feels like a blatant disregard for paying customers. The app also has a tendency to force close unexpectedly, interrupting my workflow and adding to the frustration.
Furthermore, the app's cross-device functionality is abysmal. I cannot seamlessly use it on both my iPhone and iPad because the data doesn't sync in real-time. This means I have to deal with discrepancies and inconsistencies, making it impossible to maintain a consistent experience across devices.
To summarize, this app is a buggy, ad-ridden mess with a core function that barely works. It's a waste of money and a prime example of how not to treat paying customers. I strongly advise against purchasing this app until these significant issues are addressed.

title: Elsa AI + IPhone (15) = lost your money
review: The app works fine during test period. After paid, it doesn’t work anymore. It was exactly after the free test period. All speaking lessons stopped to work, because mic doesn’t answer anymore or mic works but no sound. You are going to reinstall app several times and nothing. You are going to reset app purchase, to give all possible access… You are going to try with air pods, headphones, on speaker, no way. You are going to try app updates, nothing. You’re going to contact a support team that pretend to exist. You are going to pass some days trying and trying… until you check reviews here and online… and realized you lost your money. Sad. I have one year subscription of nothing.

title: This app is fake (I am a native speaker)
review: I am a native speaker born and raised in the United States and I downloaded this app just to test it out for fun. It CONSISTENTLY ranks me anywhere from 75-80% accuracy. I don’t speak any sort of dialect either, I am from the capitol city of Kansas— my accent is one of the most neutral in the entire county and almost everyone here outside of minority groups speaks GAE— so I know it wasn’t because I speak in a non-standard way, the AI is just extremely flawed apparently. Additionally, the AI reads the sentences in American English, like there is absolutely no reason it should be telling me I don’t sound fully native, would not recommend!
